title: DORA Art.24 — Digital operational-resilience testing: test-plan execution attestation
exclusions: Does NOT assess the quality of the test (not a TLPT scope tool); attests that the annual testing programme executed on time and the per-test evidence is present.
evidence: Saved search 'dora_art24_testing_attest' (daily) emits per test: test_id, test_type, scope_service, planned_date, actual_run_date, pass_fail, finding_count, evidence_complete (boolean), to the compliance_summary index.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.24 requires an established digital operational-resilience testing programme; evidencing that the plan executed with per-test evidence is the direct control output.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that DORA Art.24 (Digital operational-resilience testing) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.3.45: DORA Art.24 — Digital operational-resilience testing: test-plan execution attestation.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.3.45' running on sourcetype test:plan_execution and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.5.29 Information security during disruption — related; DR scope overlaps with the testing programme.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that ISO/IEC 27001 A.5.29 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.3.45: DORA Art.24 — Digital operational-resilience testing: test-plan execution attestation.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.3.45' running on sourcetype test:plan_execution and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST 800-53 CA-8 Penetration Testing — contributing evidence for the penetration-test subset of the plan.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST 800-53 CA-8 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.3.45: DORA Art.24 — Digital operational-resilience testing: test-plan execution attestation.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.3.45' running on sourcetype test:plan_execution and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Removing a planned test's execution record from index=testing past its planned_date+14d causes the UC to flag evidence_complete=0 on the next daily run.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A test with a recorded actual_run_time within the tolerance window reports evidence_complete=1, on_time=1.

dataSources: `index=testing` sourcetype=test:plan_execution (BAS/TLPT/DR runs); `index=ticketing` sourcetype=jira:issue (findings tickets); test-plan register `dora_test_plan.csv`.
spl:
| inputlookup dora_test_plan.csv
| eval planned_date_et=strptime(planned_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| join type=outer test_id
  [search index=testing sourcetype=test:plan_execution
   | stats latest(_time) AS actual_run_time latest(pass_fail) AS pass_fail sum(finding_count) AS finding_count BY test_id]
| eval evidence_complete=if(isnotnull(actual_run_time),1,0)
| eval on_time=if(actual_run_time<=planned_date_et+86400*14,1,0)
| table test_id test_type scope_service planned_date actual_run_time pass_fail finding_count evidence_complete on_time
description: Pivots the DORA test-plan register against the actual execution telemetry from the BAS/TLPT/DR tooling. Each planned test either has matching execution evidence within a 14-day tolerance or it does not — the latter is a direct Art.24 non-conformance.
value: Gives the CISO and the ICT risk committee a single-query attestation that the operational-resilience testing programme executed as planned, replacing an email-based reconciliation that typically slips by weeks.
implementation: (1) Maintain dora_test_plan.csv as the approved annual programme; (2) normalise execution output from BAS/DR tools to sourcetype=test:plan_execution; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) evidence_complete=0 past planned_date + 14d escalates to CISO; (5) per-quarter report to Board; (6) missing TLPT for significant entities escalates immediately. Indexes required: `index=testing`, `index=ticketing`.
visualization: Calendar heatmap of test executions, bar chart of findings per scope service, single value 'tests past due'. Key fields: `actual_run_time`, `evidence_complete`, `finding_count`, `on_time`, `pass_fail`, `planned_date`.
knownFalsePositives: Register changes during onboarding, contract renewals, or planned vendor consolidation.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=testing` sourcetype=test:plan_execution (BAS/TLPT/DR runs); `index=ticketing` sourcetype=jira:issue (findings tickets); test-plan register `dora_test_plan.csv`..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain dora_test_plan.csv as the approved annual programme; (2) normalise execution output from BAS/DR tools to sourcetype=test:plan_execution; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) evidence_complete=0 past planned_date + 14d escalates to CISO; (5) per-quarter report to Board; (6) missing TLPT for significant entities escalates immediately.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup dora_test_plan.csv
| eval planned_date_et=strptime(planned_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| join type=outer test_id
  [search index=testing sourcetype=test:plan_execution
   | stats latest(_time) AS actual_run_time latest(pass_fail) AS pass_fail sum(finding_count) AS finding_count BY test_id]
| eval evidence_complete=if(isnotnull(actual_run_time),1,0)
| eval on_time=if(actual_run_time<=planned_date_et+86400*14,1,0)
| table test_id test_type scope_service planned_date actual_run_time pass_fail finding_count evidence_complete on_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**DORA Art.24 — Digital operational-resilience testing: test-plan execution attestation** — Gives the CISO and the ICT risk committee a single-query attestation that the operational-resilience testing programme executed as planned, replacing an email-based reconciliation that typically slips by weeks.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=testing` sourcetype=test:plan_execution (BAS/TLPT/DR runs); `index=ticketing` sourcetype=jira:issue (findings tickets); test-plan register `dora_test_plan.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **planned_date_et** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **evidence_complete** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **on_time** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

Output fields: actual_run_time, evidence_complete, finding_count, on_time, pass_fail, planned_date, planned_date_et, scope_service.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Calendar heatmap of test executions, bar chart of findings per scope service, single value 'tests past due'.
grandmaExplanation: We pivots the DORA test-plan register against the actual execution telemetry from the BAS/TLPT/DR tooling.
